claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 10 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility criteria to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. To help me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have a child, Alex, who's 10 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask a few questions.

Do you live with Alex in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I live in the UK, but Alex doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I understand. Since Alex doesn't live with you, I need to ask: do you contribute financially towards Alex's upkeep (such as for food, clothes, pocket money, or accommodation)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do contribute towards Alex's upkeep - I help out with things like clothes and other bits and pieces.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To determine your eligibility, I need to understand the amount you're contributing.

Do you contribute at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment would be? (Currently, Child Benefit is £25.60 per week for the eldest or only child, which is about £111 per month or £1,331 per year)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, I see. No, I don't contribute that much. I give about £20 a week.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I have one more important question to help me assess your eligibility:

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex (such as the person Alex lives with)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for answering all my questions. Let me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex based on the information you've provided.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why: To qualify for Child Benefit when a child doesn't live with you, you need to be contributing at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment towards their upkeep. The current Child Benefit rate for an eldest or only child is £25.60 per week, and you're contributing approximately £20 per week, which is less than this amount.

However, if you were to increase your contributions to at least £25.60 per week (or the equivalent in kind, such as through clothes, food, pocket money, or financial contributions towards accommodation), you could become eligible for Child Benefit for Alex, provided no one else is claiming it.

If you have any questions or if your circumstances change in the future, you can contact the Child Benefit Office for further guidance.

reasonings: According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify if you're responsible for a child under 16 and live in the UK. You'll usually be responsible for a child if either: (1) you live with them, or (2) you're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or the equivalent in kind) towards looking after them - for example on food, clothes or pocket money. In this case, Alex is 10 years old and you live in the UK, which meets the basic criteria. However, Alex does not live with you, and you contribute approximately £20 per week towards Alex's upkeep. The Child Benefit rate for an eldest or only child is £25.60 per week, and your contribution of £20 per week is less than this amount. Therefore, you do not meet the requirement of paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards Alex's upkeep, and you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 10), who does not live with them. While the claimant contributes £20 per week towards Alex's upkeep, this is less than the Child Benefit rate of £25.60 per week for an eldest/only child. Since the claimant doesn't live with Alex and doesn't contribute at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount, they do not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
